risk_name: Electrolyzer Manufacturing Capacity Shortfall Causing Delivery Delay (>12 months)

description.summary: Global electrolyzer manufacturing capacity cannot meet aggregate demand during the project's procurement window, causing delivery delays of 6-18 months. PEM projects face higher risk due to OEM concentration (effectively duopoly at >100 MW).
description.detailed: Electrolyzer manufacturing is scaling from ~10 GW/year (2023) toward 50+ GW/year (2030), but demand is scaling even faster. During industry boom periods, OEM order books fill 2-3 years in advance. For a PEM project ordering 100+ MW, only Siemens Energy and ITM Power can currently supply >20 MW modules (Plug Power emerging at 10 MW). If their manufacturing slots are fully booked, the project faces delivery delays or must switch OEM, requiring BOP redesign. ITM Power has pioneered a capacity reservation system (refundable deposit for manufacturing slot) that provides demand visibility — this is an emerging best practice.
description.root_cause: Mismatch between rapid hydrogen demand growth and electrolyzer manufacturing capacity ramp-up. PEM manufacturing is concentrated (2-3 OEMs); Alkaline is diversified (10+ OEMs).
trigger_events: OEM announces order book full for target delivery year
OEM gigafactory ramp-up misses publicly stated milestones
>3 competing projects >100 MW announce FID within same 6-month window

assessment.probability_rationale: IEA and BNEF track manufacturing capacity vs. announced project pipeline. Current projections show sufficient capacity through 2028, tightening 2029-2030. PEM OEM concentration increases probability vs Alkaline.
assessment.impact_rationale: 12-24 month delivery delay at 100 MW: €20-40M lost revenue, offtake default risk, workforce demobilization. BOP redesign if switching OEM: €5-10M.
assessment.detectability_rationale: OEM quarterly reports and public order book announcements provide partial visibility. Capacity reservation agreements improve detectability significantly.
assessment.assessment_method: IEA/BNEF manufacturing capacity analysis + Gold Dataset OEM tracking

schedule.description: Critical path delay. BOP and civil works idled while awaiting electrolyzer delivery.

cost.description: €20-40M: lost revenue + demobilization + offtake penalties for 12-month delay at 100 MW.

preventive_actions.description: Reserve manufacturing capacity via slot reservation agreement with OEM at pre-FID stage (5-10% deposit)
Qualify backup OEM during FEED. Design BOP for stack-agnostic interface.

monitoring_indicators.indicator: OEM order book status for target delivery year

incident_descriptions: Normand'Hy (GA-PR-001): 12 PEM modules from Siemens Energy Berlin gigafactory (3 GW/year). 9 of 12 delivered on schedule as of late 2025. Demonstrates that gigafactory-scale manufacturing can meet delivery commitments.

REFHYNE II (GA-PR-008): ITM Power capacity reservation signed December 2023, formal contract August 2024. Manufacturing 2025-2026 for 2027 delivery. The 18-month gap between reservation and delivery illustrates the typical PEM procurement timeline.
Galp Sines (GA-PR-010): Plug Power GenEco 10 MW modules (10 units). First module delivered October 2025. Plug Power is a newer entrant to large-scale PEM — delivery track record is being established.
evidence.lessons_learned: Capacity reservation is the single most effective mitigation. PEM OEM capacity is the binding constraint for the industry through 2030. Alkaline offers supply chain security through OEM diversity + Chinese supply option.
